plan-phase.name: New L2 schemas in place
plan-phase.description: Create the five new schemas (adrs, spec-reviews, features, framework-gaps, layer-plans) in .project/schemas/. Existing schemas untouched.
exit_criteria: .project/schemas/decisions.schema.json exists and is valid draft-07
.project/schemas/spec-reviews.schema.json exists and is valid draft-07
.project/schemas/features.schema.json exists and is valid draft-07
.project/schemas/framework-gaps.schema.json exists and is valid draft-07
.project/schemas/layer-plans.schema.json exists and is valid draft-07

plan-phase.name: Seed initial L2/L3 artifacts
plan-phase.description: Populate each new block with seed data representing the current state of the consumer migration arc — three open decisions, one spec-review target, one feature with story decomposition, six framework gaps, one layer plan.
exit_criteria: decisions.json contains DEC-0001 through DEC-0003 in proposed state
spec-reviews.json contains REVIEW-001 in not-started state
features.json contains FEAT-001 (consumer migration epic) in proposed state
framework-gaps.json contains FGAP-001 through FGAP-006 in identified state
layer-plans.json contains PLAN-001 in draft state
every data file validates against its schema via AJV

plan-phase.name: Run design review of jit-agents-spec.md
plan-phase.description: Spawn a fresh-context independent reviewer subagent against docs/planning/jit-agents-spec.md with the scope declared in REVIEW-001. Findings populate REVIEW-001.findings and drive DEC-0001/0002/0003 state from proposed toward decided where evidence supports it.
exit_criteria: REVIEW-001 advances to complete
Every finding in REVIEW-001.findings reaches a terminal state (resolved/wontfix/duplicate)
Findings that generate new decisions are linked via produces_decision
REVIEW-001.clean is true

plan-phase.name: User-authored transitions on gating decisions
plan-phase.description: User reviews DEC-0001/0002/0003 in light of the design-review findings and advances each to enacted or requests revision. All open-to-enacted transitions require user authority per the lifecycle metadata. Until each gating decision is enacted, FEAT-001 cannot advance from proposed to approved.
exit_criteria: DEC-0001 in enacted state with enacted_by=user
DEC-0002 in enacted state with enacted_by=user
DEC-0003 in enacted state with enacted_by=user

plan-phase.name: FEAT-001 story execution
plan-phase.description: User advances FEAT-001 to approved. Each story within FEAT-001 executes in dependency order, starting with the decision-enacted boundary changes (STORY-001/002/003 for model-pin, thinking-seam, parseModelSpec move) and proceeding to the consumer call-site migration (STORY-004/005), bundled YAML updates (STORY-006), duplicate removal (STORY-007), empirical verification (STORY-008), and issue closeout (STORY-009).
exit_criteria: FEAT-001 in complete state
All nine stories in FEAT-001.stories in complete state
The five existing issues named in blocks_resolved are closed in issues.json or migrated to their scope-owned registries
pi-workflows and pi-behavior-monitors import agent infrastructure from pi-jit-agents

plan-phase.name: Retroactive issue migration
plan-phase.description: Migrate existing issues.json entries into scope-owned finding registries. Each issue gets a layer tag (L2/L3/L4) and is moved to the appropriate owner — spec-reviews for L2 design issues, features for L3 scope conflicts, features[].findings for L4 implementation defects. Global issues.json becomes a derived view. Depends on FGAP-002 and FGAP-003 landing for proper enforcement, but can be hand-enacted in the interim.
exit_criteria: Every issue in issues.json has a layer tag or has been moved to a scope-owned registry
issues.json is either empty or contains only entries not yet classified

plan-phase.name: Framework gap closure
plan-phase.description: Address the six framework gaps (FGAP-001 through FGAP-006) so the hand-enacted L1–L5 structure becomes natively expressible. Each gap becomes a feature of its own with stories and tasks. Order is informed by priority (P1 first) and by what the remaining phases need.
exit_criteria: FGAP-001 through FGAP-006 all in closed or wontfix state
pi-project natively supports nested blocks, scoped findings, materialized views, authorship attestation, state-machine validation, and schema versioning
produces: framework substrate for the layered model
